  "concepts": [
    {"id": "c_open_data", "name": "Open data", "aliases": ["open government data", "government open data"], "description": "A systematic approach to making Federal data accessible and usable to the public.", "grounding_span": "systematic approach to open data", "location": "INTRODUCTION"},
    {"id": "c_data_asset", "name": "Data asset", "aliases": ["data assets"], "description": "A logically grouped collection of structured or semi-structured data elements or data sets.", "grounding_span": "a collection of data elements or data sets that may be grouped together", "location": "DEFINITIONS"},
    {"id": "c_public_data_asset", "name": "Public data asset", "aliases": [], "description": "A data asset maintained by the Federal Government that has been or may be released to the public.", "grounding_span": "that has been, or may be, released to the public, including any data asset, or part thereof, subject", "location": "DEFINITIONS"},
    {"id": "c_open_gov_data_asset", "name": "Open Government data asset", "aliases": [], "description": "A public data asset that is machine-readable, in an open format, unencumbered, and based on an open standard.", "grounding_span": "open Government data asset", "location": "DEFINITIONS"},
    {"id": "c_open_format", "name": "Open format", "aliases": [], "description": "A platform-independent, machine-readable file format maintained at no cost with no reuse restrictions.", "grounding_span": "Maintain each agency data asset in an open format", "location": "SCOPE AND APPLICABILITY"},
    {"id": "c_open_license", "name": "Open license", "aliases": [], "description": "A legal guarantee that a data asset is available at no cost and without reuse restrictions.", "grounding_span": "Open license means", "location": "DEFINITIONS"},
    {"id": "c_open_standard", "name": "Open standard", "aliases": [], "description": "A standard freely available to the public to access, use, and share.", "grounding_span": "Open standard means a standard that is freely available to the public to access, use, and share.", "location": "DEFINITIONS"},
    {"id": "c_machine_readable", "name": "Machine-readable data", "aliases": ["machine readability"], "description": "Data in a format easily processed by a computer without human intervention while preserving semantics.", "grounding_span": "(A) machine-readable", "location": "DEFINITIONS"},
    {"id": "c_metadata", "name": "Metadata", "aliases": [], "description": "Structural or descriptive information about data such as content, format, source, and provenance.", "grounding_span": "Metadata means", "location": "DEFINITIONS"},
    {"id": "c_comp_inventory", "name": "Comprehensive data inventory", "aliases": [], "description": "An agency-wide inventory accounting for all data assets in the possession of the agency.", "grounding_span": "comprehensive data inventory", "location": "Section 4(a)"},
    {"id": "c_federal_data_catalog", "name": "Federal Data Catalog", "aliases": ["Data.gov"], "description": "A single public online interface maintained by GSA as a point of entry for sharing agency data assets.", "grounding_span": "Federal Data Catalog", "location": "Section 5(b)"},
    {"id": "c_open_data_plan", "name": "Open data plan", "aliases": [], "description": "A plan, incorporated into the IRM Strategic Plan, describing how an agency fulfills its open data obligations.", "grounding_span": "open data plan", "location": "Section 6"},
    {"id": "c_public_engagement", "name": "Public engagement", "aliases": ["public collaboration"], "description": "Engaging with the public in using public data assets and encouraging collaboration.", "grounding_span": "Public engagement", "location": "SCOPE AND APPLICABILITY"},
    {"id": "c_open_by_default", "name": "Open by default", "aliases": [], "description": "The principle that government data should be maintained in open format and open license absent specific concerns.", "grounding_span": "Open by default", "location": "SCOPE AND APPLICABILITY"},
    {"id": "c_cdo", "name": "Chief Data Officer", "aliases": ["CDO", "CDOs"], "description": "Agency official responsible for implementing the data management provisions of this guidance.", "grounding_span": "Chief Data Officers (CDOs)", "location": "INTRODUCTION"},
    {"id": "c_cdo_council", "name": "CDO Council", "aliases": [], "description": "Interagency council of Chief Data Officers established under the OPEN Government Data Act.", "grounding_span": "and a CDO Council", "location": "INTRODUCTION"},
    {"id": "c_data_governance_body", "name": "Data Governance Body", "aliases": [], "description": "Agency body of senior staff and technical experts that sets policy on data-related topics.", "grounding_span": "Data Governance Body", "location": "Section 2(b)"},
    {"id": "c_evidence_building", "name": "Evidence-building", "aliases": ["evidence-based policymaking"], "description": "Federal activities that develop and use evidence to inform decision making.", "grounding_span": "evidence-building", "location": "INTRODUCTION"},
    {"id": "c_mosaic_effect", "name": "Mosaic effect", "aliases": [], "description": "The risk that combining a data asset with other information compromises a protected interest.", "grounding_span": "Mosaic effect means", "location": "DEFINITIONS"},
    {"id": "c_reidentification", "name": "Reidentification", "aliases": [], "description": "The process by which deidentified information can be turned back into PII.", "grounding_span": "reidentification risk associated with the dissemination of any deidentified data", "location": "Section 5(b)(iii)"},
    {"id": "c_deidentified", "name": "Deidentified data", "aliases": [], "description": "Data from which identifying information has been removed but which may still be reidentifiable.", "grounding_span": "deidentified data", "location": "Section 5(b)(iii)"},
    {"id": "c_pii", "name": "Personally identifiable information", "aliases": ["PII"], "description": "Information that can distinguish or trace an individual's identity, alone or combined.", "grounding_span": "Personally identifiable information (PII)", "location": "DEFINITIONS"},
    {"id": "c_privacy", "name": "Privacy and confidentiality", "aliases": [], "description": "Protected interests weighed against dissemination of public data assets.", "grounding_span": "Privacy and Confidentiality", "location": "Section 5(b)(iii)"},
    {"id": "c_national_security", "name": "National security", "aliases": [], "description": "A protected interest; national security systems data are excluded from dissemination.", "grounding_span": "national security", "location": "Section 5(b)(iii)"},
    {"id": "c_information_security", "name": "Information security", "aliases": [], "description": "Security-related restrictions on release that agencies weigh before dissemination.", "grounding_span": "Information Security", "location": "Section 5(b)(iii)"},
    {"id": "c_intellectual_property", "name": "Intellectual property", "aliases": [], "description": "Rights (e.g., copyright under Title 17) that may restrict dissemination of a data asset.", "grounding_span": "Intellectual Property", "location": "Section 5(b)(iii)"},
    {"id": "c_foia", "name": "FOIA disclosure", "aliases": ["Freedom of Information Act"], "description": "The standard for public disclosure of records under 5 U.S.C. § 552.", "grounding_span": "exempt from disclosure under FOIA", "location": "Section 4(a)"},
    {"id": "c_sdl", "name": "Statistical disclosure limitation", "aliases": ["privacy enhancing techniques"], "description": "Techniques applied to manage and reduce reidentification risk in disseminated data.", "grounding_span": "statistical disclosure limitation", "location": "Section 5(b)(iii)"},
    {"id": "c_differential_privacy", "name": "Differential privacy", "aliases": ["noise injection"], "description": "A technique that induces known inaccuracy in data to protect privacy, subject to disclosure conditions.", "grounding_span": "noise injection or differential", "location": "Section 5(b)(iii)"},
    {"id": "c_risk_assessment", "name": "Risk assessment", "aliases": [], "description": "Established law-and-policy tools used to assess dissemination risk.", "grounding_span": "risk assessment tools", "location": "Section 5(b)(iii)"},
    {"id": "c_public_interest", "name": "Public interest", "aliases": [], "description": "The benefit served by dissemination, weighed against risks.", "grounding_span": "public interest", "location": "Section 5(b)(iii)"},
    {"id": "c_dissemination", "name": "Dissemination", "aliases": [], "description": "Government-initiated distribution of information to a non-government entity.", "grounding_span": "Dissemination means", "location": "DEFINITIONS"},
    {"id": "c_sap", "name": "Standard Application Process", "aliases": ["SAP"], "description": "Process through which RSAUs accept applications to access confidential statistical data.", "grounding_span": "Standard Application Process (SAP)", "location": "Section 5(b)(v)"},
    {"id": "c_rsau", "name": "Recognized Statistical Agencies and Units", "aliases": ["RSAUs"], "description": "Entities that accept applications to access confidential statistical data for evidence-building.", "grounding_span": "Recognized Statistical Agencies and Units (RSAUs)", "location": "Section 5(b)(v)"},
    {"id": "c_ai", "name": "Artificial intelligence", "aliases": ["AI"], "description": "Technology whose development, testing, or performance measurement can prioritize data assets.", "grounding_span": "will foster innovation in artificial intelligence and beyond", "location": "INTRODUCTION"},
    {"id": "c_interoperability", "name": "Data interoperability", "aliases": [], "description": "The capability promoted by open attributes that increases efficiency and reuse.", "grounding_span": "data interoperability, which increases operational efficiencies", "location": "Section 5(a)"},
    {"id": "c_fair", "name": "FAIR data", "aliases": ["findable accessible interoperable reusable"], "description": "Making open data and metadata findable, accessible, interoperable, and reusable.", "grounding_span": "findable, accessible, interoperable, and reusable", "location": "Section 6(h)"},
    {"id": "c_transparency", "name": "Transparency and accountability", "aliases": [], "description": "Goals advanced by public access to open Government data assets.", "grounding_span": "transparency and accountability", "location": "INTRODUCTION"},
    {"id": "c_irm_plan", "name": "IRM Strategic Plan", "aliases": ["Information Resource Management Strategic Plan"], "description": "PRA-required plan into which the open data plan is incorporated.", "grounding_span": "IRM Strategic Plan", "location": "Section 6"},
    {"id": "c_metadata_schema", "name": "Standard metadata schema", "aliases": [], "description": "The OMB-approved schema to which comprehensive data inventories must conform.", "grounding_span": "metadata schema approved by OMB", "location": "Section 4(a)(ii)"},
    {"id": "c_persistent_identifier", "name": "Persistent identifier", "aliases": ["DOI", "Digital Object Identifier"], "description": "A globally resolvable persistent URL pointing to a landing page where data can be accessed.", "grounding_span": "persistent identifier", "location": "Section 5(b)(iii)"}
  ],
  "definitions": [
    {"id": "d_agency", "term": "Agency", "verbatim_text": "any executive department, military department, Government corporation, Government controlled corporation, or other establishment in the executive branch of the Government (including the Executive Office of the President), or any independent regulatory agency, but does not include the Government Accountability Office; Federal Election Commission; the governments of the District of Columbia and of the territories and possessions of the United States, and their various subdivisions; or Government-owned contractor-operated facilities, including laboratories engaged in national defense research and production activities.", "normative_status": "statute", "as_of_date": "2025-01-15", "grounding_span": "any executive department, military department, Government corporation,", "location": "DEFINITIONS"},
    {"id": "d_data", "term": "Data", "verbatim_text": "recorded information, regardless of form or the media on which the data is recorded.", "normative_status": "statute", "as_of_date": "2025-01-15", "grounding_span": "recorded information, regardless of form or the media on which the data is", "location": "DEFINITIONS"},
    {"id": "d_data_asset", "term": "Data asset", "verbatim_text": "a collection of data elements or data sets that may be grouped together.", "normative_status": "statute", "as_of_date": "2025-01-15", "grounding_span": "a collection of data elements or data sets that may be grouped together.", "location": "DEFINITIONS"},
    {"id": "d_dissemination", "term": "Dissemination", "verbatim_text": "the government-initiated distribution of information to a non-government entity, including the public. The term 'dissemination' does not include distribution limited to Federal Government employees, intra- or interagency use or sharing of Federal information, or responses to requests for agency records under the Freedom of Information Act (5 U.S.C. § 552) or the Privacy Act (5 U.S.C. § 552a).", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2025-01-15", "grounding_span": "the government-initiated distribution of information to a non-government", "location": "DEFINITIONS"},
    {"id": "d_machine_readable", "term": "Machine-readable", "verbatim_text": "data in a format that can be easily processed by a computer without human intervention while ensuring no semantic meaning is lost.", "normative_status": "statute", "as_of_date": "2025-01-15", "grounding_span": "processed by a computer without human intervention while ensuring no semantic meaning is", "location": "DEFINITIONS"},
    {"id": "d_metadata", "term": "Metadata", "verbatim_text": "structural or descriptive information about data such as content, format, source, rights, accuracy, provenance, frequency, periodicity, granularity, publisher or responsible party, contact information, method of collection, and other descriptions.", "normative_status": "statute", "as_of_date": "2025-01-15", "grounding_span": "structural or descriptive information about data such as content, format,", "location": "DEFINITIONS"},
    {"id": "d_mosaic", "term": "Mosaic effect", "verbatim_text": "the particular risk that a data asset will allow for the compromise of a protected interest (such as privacy, national security, or law enforcement interests) when the data asset is combined with other available information.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2025-01-15", "grounding_span": "Mosaic effect means the particular risk that a data asset will allow for the compromise of a", "location": "DEFINITIONS"},
    {"id": "d_open_format", "term": "Open format", "verbatim_text": "a file format for storing digital data where that format is platform independent and machine readable, and is maintained (A) at no cost to the public; and (B) with no restrictions on copying, publishing, distributing, transmitting, citing, or adapting such format.", "normative_status": "statute", "as_of_date": "2025-01-15", "grounding_span": "Open format means a file format for storing digital data where that format is platform", "location": "DEFINITIONS"},
    {"id": "d_open_gov_data_asset", "term": "Open Government data asset", "verbatim_text": "a public data asset that is—(A) machine-readable; (B) available (or could be made available) in an open format; (C) not encumbered by restrictions, other than intellectual property rights, including under titles 17 and 35 [of the U.S. Code], that would impede the use or reuse of such asset; and (D) based on an underlying open standard that is maintained by a standards organization.", "normative_status": "statute", "as_of_date": "2025-01-15", "grounding_span": "a public data asset that is—(A) machine-readable; (B)", "location": "DEFINITIONS"},
    {"id": "d_open_license", "term": "Open license", "verbatim_text": "a legal guarantee that a data asset is made available (A) at no cost to the public; and (B) with no restrictions on copying, publishing, distributing, transmitting, citing, or adapting such asset.", "normative_status": "statute", "as_of_date": "2025-01-15", "grounding_span": "a legal guarantee that a data asset is made available (A) at no cost to the", "location": "DEFINITIONS"},
    {"id": "d_open_standard", "term": "Open standard", "verbatim_text": "a standard that is freely available to the public to access, use, and share.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2025-01-15", "grounding_span": "Open standard means a standard that is freely available to the public to access, use, and share.", "location": "DEFINITIONS"},
    {"id": "d_pii", "term": "Personally identifiable information (PII)", "verbatim_text": "information that can be used to distinguish or trace an individual's identity, either alone or when combined with other information that is linked or linkable to a specific individual.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2025-01-15", "grounding_span": "information that can be used to distinguish or trace an individual\u2019s identity, either alone or when", "location": "DEFINITIONS"},
    {"id": "d_public_data_asset", "term": "Public data asset", "verbatim_text": "a data asset, or part thereof, maintained by the Federal Government that has been, or may be, released to the public, including any data asset, or part thereof, subject to disclosure under [5 U.S.C. § 552].", "normative_status": "statute", "as_of_date": "2025-01-15", "grounding_span": "that has been, or may be, released to the public, including any data asset, or part thereof, subject", "location": "DEFINITIONS"}
  ],
  "claims": [
    {"id": "cl_strategic_asset", "claim_text": "Federal data is a valuable national resource and a strategic asset.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "Federal data is a valuable national resource and a strategic asset.", "location": "INTRODUCTION"},
    {"id": "cl_rescinds", "claim_text": "This Memorandum rescinds and replaces OMB Memorandum M-13-13, the 2013 Open Data Policy.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "This Memorandum rescinds and replaces OMB Memorandum", "location": "INTRODUCTION"},
    {"id": "cl_copyright", "claim_text": "Data and content created by Federal employees within the scope of their employment are not subject to domestic copyright protection under 17 U.S.C. § 105.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "subject to domestic copyright protection under 17 U.S.C. § 105", "location": "Section 5(a)(v)"},
    {"id": "cl_datagov_nohost", "claim_text": "Data.gov does not host or maintain any data assets directly; it provides a centralized point of entry to discover them.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "Data.gov does not host or maintain any data assets directly", "location": "Section 5(b)(i)"},
    {"id": "cl_mosaic_reid", "claim_text": "The mosaic effect can cause purportedly deidentified information to become PII through reidentification.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "cause purportedly deidentified information to become PII", "location": "Section 5(b)(iii)"},
    {"id": "cl_nss_excluded", "claim_text": "Data assets maintained in national security systems are excluded from dissemination through the Federal Data Catalog.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "excluded from dissemination through the Federal Data Catalog", "location": "Section 5(b)(iii)"},
    {"id": "cl_openness", "claim_text": "In case of doubt, openness should prevail.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "In case of doubt, openness should prevail.", "location": "Section 5(b)(iii)"}
  ],

    {"id": "s_dcat", "name": "DCAT-US 3.0", "version": "3.0", "steward": "OMB / GSA (U.S. profile of W3C DCAT)", "as_of_date": "2025-01-15", "grounding_span": "DCAT-US 3.0", "location": "Section 4(a)(ii)"},
    {"id": "s_dcat_w3c", "name": "W3C Data Catalog Vocabulary Version 3", "version": "3", "steward": "W3C", "as_of_date": null, "grounding_span": "of the W3C Data Catalog Vocabulary Version 3, known as the DCAT-US 3.0.", "location": "Section 4(a)(ii)"},

    {"type": "asserts", "from_id": "m-25-05-phase-2-implementation-of-the-evidence-act-open-gove", "to_id": "cl_strategic_asset", "grounding_span": "Federal data is a valuable national resource and a strategic asset.", "location": "INTRODUCTION"},
    {"type": "asserts", "from_id": "m-25-05-phase-2-implementation-of-the-evidence-act-open-gove", "to_id": "cl_rescinds", "grounding_span": "This Memorandum rescinds and replaces OMB Memorandum", "location": "INTRODUCTION"},
    {"type": "asserts", "from_id": "m-25-05-phase-2-implementation-of-the-evidence-act-open-gove", "to_id": "cl_copyright", "grounding_span": "subject to domestic copyright protection under 17 U.S.C. § 105", "location": "Section 5(a)(v)"},
    {"type": "asserts", "from_id": "m-25-05-phase-2-implementation-of-the-evidence-act-open-gove", "to_id": "cl_datagov_nohost", "grounding_span": "Data.gov does not host or maintain any data assets directly", "location": "Section 5(b)(i)"},
